Environmental Impact Statement, Interstate 5 Pedestrian Park, Washington State

The proposal is to build a pedestrian park over Interstate 5 in downtown Seattle, between Spring Street and University Street. This park aims to provide much-needed green space in an area lacking parks, improve pedestrian safety by separating foot traffic from vehicles, and reconnect neighborhoods divided by the freeway. The project is expected to enhance the area's aesthetic appeal and increase property values without significant negative environmental impacts. It will also serve as a community space for relaxation and social interaction, benefiting both residents and visitors.
Seattle, Washington, from Spring Street to University Street
Puget Sound Air Pollution Control Agency

The Interstate 5 Pedestrian Park project was proposed to address the lack of green spaces in downtown Seattle and to improve pedestrian safety by creating a park over the busy freeway. There was some public support for the idea, as it aimed to reconnect neighborhoods and enhance community interaction. However, concerns about funding and the feasibility of construction led to ongoing discussions and revisions of the project plans.
"The park will clearly benefit the neighborhood as a whole." — Department of Army, Corps of Engineers
"We think the project will be beneficial and encourage your proceeding with the project." — Department of Housing and Urban Development
